reasoning: Although Russia began evacuating ships and equipment from the Tartus naval facility following the December 2024 collapse of the Assad regime, fully 'abandoning' a military base by completely withdrawing all personnel and ceasing all operations by a strict March 31, 2025 deadline is incredibly difficult. Satellite images from late January and February show ongoing movements, but a complete withdrawal of every single person (as required by the stringent resolution criteria) often takes longer than anticipated or results in a skeleton crew remaining to guard assets or transfer facilities. Furthermore, the Polymarket price plunged to just 5% as of March 6. This overwhelming market consensus strongly suggests that either the evacuation timeline extends past March 31, or Russia has reached a new agreement with the transitional Syrian government to leave a token force or renegotiate their presence. Given this, the probability of a complete, absolute abandonment by the end of March is very low.
